mcqs.question: Who developed Java programming language?
mcqs.explanation: Java was developed by Sun Microsystems in 1995. Later Oracle acquired Sun Microsystems in 2010.

mcqs.question: What is the file extension of Java source code?
mcqs.explanation: Java source files have .java extension. .class files contain bytecode, .jar files are Java archives.

mcqs.question: Which component executes Java bytecode?
mcqs.explanation: JVM (Java Virtual Machine) executes Java bytecode. JDK includes compiler, JRE provides runtime environment.

mcqs.question: What is the size of a byte data type in Java?
mcqs.explanation: byte is 8-bit signed integer with range -128 to 127.

mcqs.question: Which of these is NOT a Java primitive data type?
mcqs.explanation: String is a class (object) in Java, not a primitive data type. Primitive types are: byte, short, int, long, float, double, char, boolean.

mcqs.question: What will be the output: System.out.println(10/3);?
mcqs.explanation: Both operands are integers, so integer division is performed, discarding the remainder.

mcqs.question: Which loop is guaranteed to execute at least once?
mcqs.explanation: do-while loop executes the body first, then checks the condition, guaranteeing at least one execution.

mcqs.question: What is the default value of boolean in Java?
mcqs.explanation: boolean default value is false when used as instance variable. Local variables must be explicitly initialized.

mcqs.question: Which keyword is used to inherit a class in Java?
mcqs.explanation: The 'extends' keyword is used for class inheritance. 'implements' is for interface implementation.

mcqs.question: What is the return type of a constructor?
mcqs.explanation: Constructors have no return type, not even void. They have same name as the class.

mcqs.question: Which of these is the correct way to create an object?
mcqs.explanation: Objects are created using 'new' keyword followed by constructor call. Syntax: ClassName variable = new Constructor();

mcqs.question: What is the range of char in Java?
mcqs.explanation: char is 16-bit unsigned integer representing Unicode characters, range 0 to 65535.

mcqs.question: Which operator is used to access members of an object?
mcqs.explanation: Dot operator (.) is used to access members (fields, methods) of an object in Java.

mcqs.question: What is the parent class of all classes in Java?
mcqs.explanation: Object class is the root class in Java. All classes implicitly extend java.lang.Object.

mcqs.question: Which keyword is used to prevent method overriding?
mcqs.explanation: final keyword prevents method overriding. A final method cannot be overridden in subclasses.

mcqs.question: What is polymorphism in Java?
mcqs.back: The ability of an object to take many forms. Achieved through method overloading (compile-time) and method overriding (runtime).
mcqs.explanation: Polymorphism allows one interface to be used for different data types. Runtime polymorphism achieved through inheritance and overriding.

mcqs.question: Which access modifier makes members accessible only within the same package?
mcqs.explanation: Default (package-private) access means members are visible only within the same package.

mcqs.question: What is encapsulation?

options.text: Hiding data and methods in a single unit

mcqs.correctAnswer: Hiding data and methods in a single unit
mcqs.explanation: Encapsulation bundles data (variables) and methods together, restricting direct access to some components.

mcqs.question: Which of these is used to exit from a loop?
mcqs.explanation: break keyword terminates the loop immediately. continue skips current iteration.

mcqs.question: What is the size of long data type?
